mission.title: flagger: Interaction of HPA + progressDeadlineSeconds flag can cause canary failure during peak times
mission.description: Interaction of HPA + progressDeadlineSeconds flag can cause canary failure during peak times. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify flagger troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flagger deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger -n flagger --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flagger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger
```
Hi guys, we are having the following issue, currently. What is the suggested solution for this case?

### Describe the issue

Flagger doesn't progress the canary while the number of running pods is below expected -> OK.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Interaction of HPA + progressDeadlineSeconds flag can cause
steps.description:
This pull request adds the `spec.analysis.canaryReadyThreshold` for configuring the percentage of canary workloads that must be available/ready before rollout can continue.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Interaction of HPA + progressDeadlineSeconds flag is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger -n flagger --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This pull request adds the `spec.analysis.canaryReadyThreshold` for configuring the percentage of canary workloads that must be available/ready before rollout can continue.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
